prompt: Why is one kilogram of iron heavier than one kilogram of cotton?
response:
One kilogram of iron and one kilogram of cotton both weigh the same. This is because weight is the force which is caused by gravity acting upon an object, and is calculated with the formula:

Weight (W) = Mass (m) x Acceleration due to gravity (g). 

If both objects have a mass of 1 kilogram, then according to the above mentioned formula, both objects should also have the same weight.

prompt: ¿ Cual es la formula para el movimiento uniformemente acelerado en física?
response:
La fórmula para calcular la distancia recorrida para un movimiento uniformemente acelerado es:

`d = v₀t + ½at²`

Donde:
* d: es la distancia recorrida por el objeto en movimiento.
* v₀: es la velocidad inicial del objeto en movimiento.
* t: es el tiempo transcurrido desde el inicio del movimiento.
* a: es la aceleración del objeto en movimiento.

response:
#!/bin/bash

# Prompt user to enter password
echo "Enter password to unlock encrypted ZFS dataset:"
read -s password

# Use entered password to unlock and mount the dataset
zfs load-key rpool/encrypted <<< "$password"
zfs mount rpool/encrypted

echo "Encrypted ZFS dataset successfully mounted."

prompt: Using C# and LINQ, please show me an example of how to sort a collection of objects based on one of their properties, and returning only unique results.
response:
Sure:

`documents.GroupBy(p => p.Name).Select(g => g.First()).ToList()`

This code is useful when you know that documents with the same names are duplicates. So you take only the first one.

You can also use:

`documents.OrderBy(o => o.Name).Distinct()`

But you will have to implement the IEquatable interface to define how to compare documents.

prompt: ¿Cuando es correcto el uso de la palabra "echo" frente a "hecho"?

Es importante recordar que la palabra "echo" viene del verbo ECHAR, el cual se refiere a la acción de tirar, dejar caer, depositar, verter o expulsar alguna cosa.

Por ejemplo: Vale, ya echo la basura en el contenedor.

Por el contrario, la palabra "hecho" viene del verbo HACER, es decir, realizar o ejecutar una acción, un movimiento o una tarea.

Por ejemplo: ¿Has hecho lo que te dije?
